project_identity.name: mintenvoy
project_identity.domain: Desktop API client for composing and sending HTTP requests, built on Electron's three-process model (main / preload / renderer) with a reusable headless UI-primitive library — Icon, Dropdown, Modal, Toast, Tabs.
project_identity.stack: TypeScript, React 19, Electron multi-process (main / preload / renderer), zustand state, Radix UI primitives, undici HTTP, electron-store persistence, electron-updater auto-update; bundled by electron-vite + Vite, packaged by electron-builder.

architecture_rules.title: Process Boundaries
architecture_rules.description: Electron's three-process security model: a Node.js main process, a contextIsolation-safe preload bridge, and a browser-only React renderer — each with a fixed import boundary.

rules.text: main may use Node/Electron freely; it never imports renderer code
preload is the only bridge — it exposes APIs to the renderer via contextBridge and depends on neither renderer UI nor main internals
renderer depends only on browser/React APIs and preload-exposed window globals — never on Node, Electron, or main
contextBridge exposure runs only under a process.contextIsolated guard and is wrapped in try/catch

code_examples.label: CORRECT
code_examples.code:
// renderer — reach the platform via a preload-exposed global
const versions = window.electron.process.versions
code_examples.annotation: renderer talks to the platform only through preload globals

code_examples.label: WRONG
code_examples.code:
// renderer — FORBIDDEN: direct Node/Electron import
import { ipcRenderer } from 'electron'
code_examples.annotation: renderer must never import Node/Electron directly

architecture_rules.title: Renderer Tier Organization
architecture_rules.description: The renderer is a small atomic-design system; component tiers and the leaf lib layer import in one direction only.

rules.text: renderer component tiers flow downward only: organisms → molecules → atoms; no sibling-tier or upward imports
renderer lib (cx, icons-glue, toastStore, settingsStore, tabsStore, requestSpec) is leaf-level — components depend on lib, lib depends on nothing renderer-external
requestSpec is imported by tabsStore but stays a pure data module — no component imports

code_examples.label: EXAMPLE
code_examples.code:
src/
├── main/        # Node.js main process — BrowserWindow, app lifecycle, IPC host
├── preload/     # contextIsolation-safe bridge (contextBridge → window globals)
└── renderer/src/
    ├── components/
    │   ├── atoms/      # Icon
    │   ├── molecules/  # Dropdown, Modal, Toast (Radix); Tabs (hand-rolled WAI-ARIA)
    │   └── organisms/  # Shell, Titlebar, Sidebar, PaneSplit, Statusbar, Divider, TabBar
    ├── lib/    # cx, icons-glue, toastStore, settingsStore, tabsStore, requestSpec
    └── styles/ # tokens.css design tokens

architecture_rules.title: Import & Path Rules
architecture_rules.description: Cross-module renderer imports resolve through a path alias, never deep relative chains; Node/Electron stay out of the renderer entirely.

rules.text: renderer imports cross-module code via the @renderer path alias rather than deep relative paths
renderer modules import no Node/Electron APIs

code_quality_standards.title: Type Safety
code_quality_standards.description: TypeScript strict mode across both node and web configs; untyped boundaries narrow rather than cast.

rules.text: TypeScript strict mode is on (tsconfig strict) — no implicit any
type-check passes for both configs before task completion (npm run typecheck:node && npm run typecheck:web)
prefer unknown + a type guard over any at untyped boundaries

code_examples.label: CORRECT
code_examples.annotation: narrow unknown through a type guard

code_examples.label: WRONG
code_examples.code: const data = JSON.parse(raw) as any  // bypasses type safety

code_quality_standards.title: Error Handling
code_quality_standards.description: Boundary lookups degrade gracefully; bridge exposure logs on failure rather than swallowing.

rules.text: boundary lookups degrade gracefully instead of throwing (resolveIcon returns a fallback entry)
contextBridge exposure is wrapped in try/catch and logs on failure rather than swallowing
never swallow errors — handle, re-throw, or log with a reason; no empty catch blocks

code_quality_standards.title: Naming Conventions
code_quality_standards.description: Components PascalCase one-per-file; lib helpers and stores camelCase; styles in sibling .css.

rules.text: components in PascalCase, one per file (Icon.tsx, Dropdown.tsx)
lib helpers and stores in camelCase modules (cx.ts, toastStore.ts)

code_quality_standards.title: Testing Requirements
code_quality_standards.description: Vitest unit + Playwright component tests, co-located by tier.

rules.text: co-located tests under __tests__/ next to the code, split .test.tsx (Vitest) and .ct.tsx (Playwright CT)
cover the acceptance criteria including edge and error paths

code_quality_standards.title: Documentation & Code Discovery
code_quality_standards.description: New code is documented; structural code exploration routes through the codebase-memory graph, not raw file reads.

rules.text: all new functions/variables carry clear documentation

structural code queries route through codebase-memory-mcp tools (search_graph / trace_path / get_code_snippet / search_code / query_graph) — NOT raw Read/Grep/Glob over source files; CBM hooks block raw discovery at PreToolUse on the first match per session
docs/ is LLM-context-source first, dev-greppable second; concern prose lives in docs/<pkg>/<concern>/index.md; structural metadata stays in CBM, never embedded in docs/

code_quality_standards.title: Simplicity & Reuse
code_quality_standards.description: Keep changes minimal and reuse before building.

rules.text: SOLID, DRY, KISS — single responsibility; don't repeat logic 3+ times; keep it simple
search the codebase for an existing utility/helper/component before writing anything generic

always_universal.text: Validate external input at module boundaries; trust internal code
Handle both success and error paths for every fallible operation
Read a file before modifying it

always_project_specific.text: Shared UI state lives in module-level zustand stores (toastStore, settingsStore, tabsStore) — one instance each
Mutate state only through store actions

Shell view state (theme, accent, mstyle, sidebarWidth, paneRatio, sidebarCollapsed) lives exclusively in settingsStore — Shell.tsx is the sole writer of the matching document.documentElement attrs/vars
Working-tabs lifecycle (open, dedupe, close, dirty) lives exclusively in tabsStore — TabBar.tsx is the sole subscriber wiring store actions to the Tabs molecule
Compose conditional class tokens with cx() (falsy-filtering merge)
Resolve icon names through resolveIcon — total, never throws, returns a fallback entry

never_universal.text: Never commit secrets or debug artifacts (console.log, debugger, stray print)
Never modify code outside the task scope

never_project_specific.text: Never import Node/Electron in the renderer
Never expose privileged APIs outside the preload contextBridge
Never mutate zustand state outside a store action
Never write Shell document.documentElement vars/attrs from anywhere but Shell.tsx
Never add a raw px delta to a unitless ratio (Divider ratio-valued drag hazard)
Never use inline styles — class-based styling composed with cx()

prefer_project_specific.text: Prefer wrapping Radix for new molecules (Dropdown/Modal/Toast); hand-rolled WAI-ARIA only as a documented departure (Tabs)
Prefer design tokens (CSS custom properties in tokens.css) over literal style values
Prefer the imperative toast() API for fire-and-forget toasts (wraps toastStore)
Prefer the @renderer alias over deep relative imports

domain_rules.title: Key Entities
domain_rules.description: The core domain objects of the API client and its UI shell.

rules.text: RequestSpec — the HTTP request domain model (method, url, headers, body); created blank via makeBlankRequest
Working tab — an open request in the tabs strip; lifecycle (open, dedupe, close, dirty) owned by tabsStore
Toast — a transient notification queued in toastStore, surfaced via the imperative toast() API
View state — theme, accent, mstyle, sidebarWidth, paneRatio, sidebarCollapsed; the Shell SSOT in settingsStore
UI primitives — Icon (atom); Dropdown/Modal/Toast/Tabs (molecules); Shell/Titlebar/Sidebar/PaneSplit/Statusbar/Divider/TabBar (organisms)

domain_rules.title: Domain Invariants
domain_rules.description: Lifecycle and ownership rules the domain state must hold.

rules.text: A working tab is deduped on open — re-opening the same request focuses the existing tab instead of duplicating it
A working tab carries a dirty flag tracking unsaved edits
settingsStore is the single source of truth for Shell view state; Shell.tsx is the only writer of the matching document attrs/vars
requestSpec stays a pure data module — imported by tabsStore, never by components
Every change impacts as little code as possible
Never "fix" unrelated code you happen to see
Always read a file before modifying it
Search the codebase for an existing utility/helper/component before writing anything generic or reusable
Execute tasks sequentially following the dependency graph
Read constitution.md and .devforge/memory.md before starting each task
Spec-driven flow is strict — specs are contracts; once approved, implementation must satisfy every acceptance criterion
Hard gates block: spec approval → /plan; plan approval → /breakdown; breakdown approval → /implement; ACs verified in /verify
Lint + type-check must pass on all changed files before task completion
Commits follow Conventional Commits and include the Co-Authored-By: Claude attribution trailer
